data.label: Direct
data.description: The planet or mobile astrological object is moving forward in zodiacal longitude at the chart calculation time.
data.runtime_usage: Use for calculated planetary positions whose apparent zodiacal longitude increases over time. This is the normal state for the Sun and Moon in a classical geocentric chart.
data.calculation_notes: Determine from apparent speed in degrees per day or by comparing longitude around the chart time. Keep speed magnitude in a separate field such as apparent_speed_deg_per_day.

data.label: Retrograde
data.description: The planet or mobile astrological object is moving backward in zodiacal longitude at the chart calculation time.
data.runtime_usage: Use for calculated planetary positions whose apparent zodiacal longitude decreases over time. This state can feed condition operators, accidental dignity rules, interpretation rules and expert display.
data.calculation_notes: Determine from negative apparent speed or by comparing longitude around the chart time. Do not store retrograde as a static property of the planet definition.

data.label: Stationary
data.description: The planet or mobile astrological object is nearly motionless before or after a change of apparent direction.
data.runtime_usage: Use as a generic station condition when the engine only knows that apparent speed is close to zero. Prefer a future split into stationary_direct and stationary_retrograde when the runtime can determine the outgoing direction.
data.calculation_notes: Requires a planet-specific or engine-level speed threshold. This value should be selected from apparent_speed_deg_per_day close to zero, not from zodiacal position alone.
